name: La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop
name_original: Puerto Canencia y Puerto de la Morcuera desde Miraflores de la Sierra — circular por la ruta Pinares de Canencia
description: Cycle the difficult 18.5-mile La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop, featuring challenging climbs and panoramic mountain views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Miraflores De La Sierra are set within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offering a diverse natural landscape at an elevation of 1,150 meters. The region features mountainous terrain with extensive pine and oak forests, alongside lowlands dotted with ash and holm oak trees. Cyclists encounter challenging mountain passes, such as La Morcuera and Canencia, and numerous forest tracks, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Miraflores De La Sierra The most popular touring cycling route is La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop, a 39.9 miles (64.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gains and panoramic mountain views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuente del Collado Restaurant – Camino de Cabanillas loop from Miraflores de la Sierra, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route winds through varied landscapes, including forested areas and open terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the La Morcuera Pass – La Morcuera Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Miraflores De La Sierra is defined by challenging mountain passes, extensive forest tracks, and diverse ecosystems within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter paths to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Miraflores De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Miraflores De La Sierra offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 20 options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for most cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The terrain in Miraflores De La Sierra is diverse, characterized by its mountainous environment within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You'll encounter extensive pine and oak forests, as well as lowlands with ash and holm oak trees. The region is known for challenging mountain passes like La Morcuera and Canencia, alongside countless kilometers of forest tracks, providing varied landscapes and surfaces for your ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Miraflores De La Sierra offers beautiful cycling experiences throughout the year, with each season presenting its unique charm. Summer provides cool, shaded paths through pine forests, while autumn transforms the landscape with spectacular colors from yellow ash and poplar trees. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming nature. Even winter can be appealing, with snow-capped peaks providing a dramatic backdrop, though some higher passes might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes often pass by stunning natural features. You can discover the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River, or enjoy views of the Miraflores de la Sierra reservoir. The area is also home to several significant mountain passes, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offer panoramic mountain vistas.

Q: Can I find any historical or cultural points of interest while cycling?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, many cycling routes in Miraflores De La Sierra lead past vestiges of human activity, adding a historical dimension to your ride. You might encounter old sheepfolds, shepherd huts, ancient charcoal kilns, and traditional dry stone walls. The town itself boasts a rich monumental heritage, including a notable 15th-century parish church.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging mountain passes, the extensive network of forest tracks, and the diverse ecosystems found within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Miraflores De La Sierra features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189865855/la-morcuera-and-canencia-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 64.2 km and offers significant elevation gains. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38211095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Collado Restaurant – Camino de Cabanillas loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, spanning about 46.0 km through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenges in Miraflores De La Sierra. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189865855/la-morcuera-and-canencia-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding ride with over 1,280 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40337336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a substantial climb over 25 km.

Q: Are there any recreational areas or places to rest along the routes?
A: Yes, the region offers pleasant spots for rest and refreshment. The Fuente del Cura Recreation Area, for instance, provides drinking water and is surrounded by oak trees, making it an ideal stop during your cycling excursions. Many routes also pass through charming villages where you can find cafes and other amenities.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Given its location within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and a Biosphere Reserve, Miraflores De La Sierra boasts a rich biodiversity. You might spot ibex in higher altitudes, or traces of roe deer, wild boar, and foxes in the pine and oak groves. The area is also a haven for birdwatching, with species ranging from the bridled titmouse to various eagles and vultures.

Q: Are there any routes that focus specifically on mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Miraflores De La Sierra is renowned as a gateway to challenging mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass – La Morcuera Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to tackle these iconic climbs, offering significant elevation and rewarding views. These passes, including La Morcuera and Canencia, are popular for their demanding ascents and panoramic vistas.

Q: What are the general characteristics of the touring cycling routes in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Touring cycling around Miraflores De La Sierra is defined by its challenging mountain passes, extensive forest tracks, and diverse ecosystems, all set within the protected Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The network provides options for various ability levels, from shorter paths to routes with significant elevation gains, ensuring a rich and varied cycling experience.
tips: The port of Canencia is one of the most pleasant to climb in the Sierra de Guadarrama. On any of its slopes, both from Miraflores de la Sierra and from the town of Canencia, the slopes are not too steep and the landscape is incredible.

The track that connects the ports of Morcuera and Canencia is pure fun, especially if you do it in that sense.
More at https://bigjoe.es/ruta-mtb-tres-cantos-morcuera-canencia/

It is located in the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, between the municipalities of Miraflores de la Sierra and Rascafría, in the Community of Madrid.

From Miraflores de la Sierra (1200m), the route ascends approximately 600m over 10km (average gradient 4–6%), and after reaching the summit, it descends 13km to Rascafría (1150m) with a gradient of 4.5–7%.

faqs.question: How challenging is the La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring cyclists. It features significant elevation gains, often exceeding 1,000 meters, with the most iconic climb being to La Morcuera Pass. This ascent is approximately 15 km long with an average gradient of 5%, demanding good to very good fitness. The Canencia Pass offers a gentler, but still substantial, climb.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly paved, offering smooth roads suitable for touring cycling. While the main roads are generally smooth, be prepared for significant climbs and descents that will test your endurance. Some variations of the loop might include unpaved sections, but the core route over the passes is well-maintained.

faqs.question: What scenic views and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to diverse and breathtaking landscapes. Expect panoramic mountain views, especially from the summit of La Morcuera Pass. The route also passes through the tranquil Lozoya Valley, offering views of the Embalse de Pinilla (Pinilla Reservoir) and charming traditional mountain villages like Miraflores de la Sierra and Rascafría. You'll also ride along the Morcuera–Canencia Track.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop?
faqs.answer: Starting points like Miraflores de la Sierra or Soto del Real offer convenient parking options for cyclists. These towns provide good access to the loop and amenities before you begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, towns like Miraflores de la Sierra, which is a common starting point for this loop, are generally accessible by public transport from Madrid, typically via bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most current information.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is most favorable. During these months, you'll experience pleasant temperatures and clear skies, ideal for enjoying the mountain scenery. Winter cycling can be challenging due to snow and ice on the higher passes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on the main roads and designated routes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of protected areas. For specific activities or off-road cycling, it's always best to check the official park guidelines.

faqs.question: Is the La Morcuera and Canencia Passes Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily a road cycling loop, if you plan to stop in towns or explore natural areas adjacent to the road, dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, but must be kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through charming towns such as Miraflores de la Sierra, Rascafría, and Canencia village. These towns offer various options for cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and refresh during your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this touring bicycle route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Pinares de Canencia, [CIMA MA04] Morcuera * Miraflores de la Sierra, and [CIMA MA03] Canencia * Miraflores. It also briefly touches parts of the Camino de Santiago Complutense and the Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 5.

faqs.question: Are there any tips for avoiding crowds on the route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy a quieter cycling experience, especially on the climb to La Morcuera Pass, consider riding during weekdays. The roads tend to be less busy, allowing for a more serene and focused ride through the stunning mountain scenery.
